post.description:
Authenticate with username + password and set the session cookie.

Failed attempts are rate-limited per ``(client_ip, username)`` pair to
blunt credential-stuffing. Successful logins clear the counter.
Revoke the current session server-side and clear the cookie.

schema.title: Response Me Api Auth Me Get

Redirect to OIDC provider for authentication.
Handle OIDC callback after provider authentication.

schema.title: Response List Patients Api Patients Get

get.description: Get a single patient's details.

delete.description: Delete a patient and disassociate their documents.

post.description: Upload a document into the current user's inbox for processing.
Schedule a batch of documents for later processing.

head.description:
HEAD probe used by the frontend to decide whether to render a file
viewer at all. Returns the same status codes as the GET (404 when
the file is missing on disk, 403 when the caller lacks access).

get.description:
Stream the stored document file.

Security invariants:
- ``doc.file_path`` is resolved strictly under the configured vault root.
- The outbound ``Content-Disposition`` filename is sanitised so a
  malicious filename cannot break out of the header.
- Non-admins need either patient-level access or uploader identity.

post.description:
Permanently rotate pages of a PDF document.

- degrees: rotation angle (90, 180, 270)
- pages: list of 1-based page numbers to rotate, or null/empty for all pages

Rename a document's file on disk and in the database.

The new filename is sanitised (no path separators, no ``..``, reserved
names rejected) and the target path is verified to stay under the vault
root. The file extension is locked to the original to prevent tricks
like renaming ``scan.pdf`` into ``scan.html``.
Link two documents together.

get.description: Get all linked documents for a document.

post.description: Use LLM to suggest related documents for linking.

get.description: Get AI-suggested relevant documents. Returns cached results if available.

post.description:
Edit document metadata using natural language instruction via LLM.

Uses a compact prompt to minimize token usage. For simple field changes,
the LLM returns only the changed fields as JSON.
Generate an AI-suggested filename based on document metadata.

get.description:
List failed documents with error messages for the review queue.

Restricted to admins/editors because the failure messages can expose
internal paths, provider error strings, or extracted content snippets.

post.description: Retry all failed documents. Admin-only (spawns N pipeline tasks).

delete.description: Delete a document and its file from disk. Gracefully handles in-progress processing.

post.description:
Cancel processing of a document. Requires write access.

Belt-and-braces: we both set the cooperative ``cancelled_docs`` flag
(honoured at phase boundaries inside the pipeline) and hard-cancel the
running asyncio task if one is registered. The hard cancel aborts any
in-flight ``await`` immediately (httpx POST, DB write), so the gate
slot releases and the processing chip clears without waiting for the
LLM request to finish on its own.
Reassign a document to a different patient, moving the file on disk.
Queue a document for reprocessing. Requires write access.

get.description: List medical events, optionally filtered by patient and type.

schema.title: Response List Events Api Events Get

get.description: Get a single event with its linked documents.

post.description: Link a document to a medical event.
Use LLM to suggest which medical event a document belongs to, or suggest creating a new one.

Create a lab result tied to a document.

Patient is derived from the document so the caller can't attach a lab row
to another patient's document. The document must already have a patient.

get.description:
Lab results whose document_id no longer points to an existing document.

Scoped to patients the current user has access to (same rule as the list
endpoint). Explicit patient_id narrows the result further.
Get time-series data for a specific test for charting.

List imaging studies. Mirrors the documents-list shape: search,
filters, sort, paginated results with ``total`` count.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search across body part, institution, referring physician, study description
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Search across body part, institution, referring physician, study description

get.description:
Serve a frame as PNG (default) or raw DICOM.

``wc`` and ``ww`` override the DICOM file's WindowCenter / WindowWidth
so MRI users can adjust contrast / brightness from the viewer without
re-decoding pixel data on the client. Both must be supplied together;
otherwise the file's own VOI LUT is used (or a default min-max
normalisation when none is present).

List the auxiliary files (DICOMDIR, JPEG previews, LOCKFILE, etc.)
that came in the same zip bundle as this study's DICOM frames.

Returns ``[{name, size, kind}]`` entries the UI can render and link to
via the ``GET .../bundle-file/{name}`` route.

Download a bundle file by name (the same string returned by the
list endpoint). Path traversal is rejected via ``safe_vault_join``.
Documents linked to this imaging study (e.g. a radiology report PDF).

post.description: Link an existing document (e.g. a radiology report) to this study.

delete.description: Remove a document_links row. Validates the link belongs to this study.

post.description:
Attach a radiology report PDF to an imaging study.

Two flows:
  - **Link existing**: pass ``?document_id=<id>`` (or JSON body
    ``{"document_id": <id>}``) — the study's parent document is
    repointed at that PDF; the placeholder is deleted.
  - **Upload new**: post a multipart ``file=<pdf>``. The PDF lands
    in the inbox like any other upload and is processed by the
    standard pipeline; on completion the placeholder is replaced
    by the new document.

PDF-only is enforced for uploads. The caller must have write access
to the study's patient.
Ask the configured General LLM to propose merge groups. Does NOT execute any merge.
Start the pipeline watcher at runtime.
Stop the pipeline watcher at runtime.

get.description: Get recent application logs from the in-memory buffer.
Get audit log entries (admin only).
Download a SQLite backup of the database.
List files in the scheduled-backup directory, newest-first.
Download a single scheduled-backup file.

post.description:
Trigger a backup immediately using the configured scope. Returns the
written filename.

get.description:
List server-side sessions with user + activity details.

By default shows only sessions that are still usable (not revoked, not
expired). Pass ``include_revoked=true`` to include the rest for audit.

delete.description:
Revoke a session. The owner of that session is effectively logged out
on their next request.

post.description:
Move a file (or imaging study folder) within the vault and keep the
matching document record's ``file_path`` in sync.

Use case: a file was misclassified by date / event and the user wants
to drag it into the right folder without losing the document reference
(so document-detail links, OCR cache, lab results etc. all keep
working). The DB row is updated atomically with the disk move; if the
destination already exists or the access check fails the move is
rejected.

get.description: Check if initial setup is needed (no users exist).

post.description: Create the first admin user and first patient.

DocumentUpdate.title: DocumentUpdate
DocumentUpdate.description: Partial update payload. Unset fields are left untouched.

TestProviderRequest.title: TestProviderRequest
TestProviderRequest.description:
Test-connection request.

Either pass ``provider_id`` (tests the persisted entry with that id) or
pass ``provider`` (an inline, possibly-unsaved entry). When both are set
the inline entry wins — this lets the UI test pending edits before the
user clicks Save.
